title: Charlie Kirk Eyes Closed Before Death Reaction Image
about: The Charlie Kirk Eyes Closed Before Death Reaction Image refers to an image of deceased right-wing organizer Charlie Kirk moments before he was fatally shot at Utah Valley University in September 2025, in which he has his eyes closed and is seen wearing a white T-shirt reading, "Freedom." The image was a screencap from the graphic viral video of his death that spread on Twitter / X and Reddit on the day of the shooting. In the following weeks, the image of Charlie Kirk with his eyes closed before being shot was used as a reaction image and caption meme on X. It was commonly paired with captions about failing or doing something regrettable, causing an unhappy or guilty feeling in the meme's subject. A notable example used the caption, "Just spent $20 to get Wendy's delivered," and it sparked viral discourse about the image's controversial use in memes. Many expressed a belief that the use of the image was absurd and disrespectful, given its context.
origin:
On September 10th, 2025, conservative activist Charlie Kirk was shot and killed at Utah Valley University.[1] That day, a graphic video of Kirk's death, in which he is shown being shot in the neck while speaking at the college, spread on social media websites like X.[2]

Also on September 10th, X[3] user @Gabriel_Hatay tweeted a screenshot from the video, showing Kirk with his eyes closed moments before the shot, paired with the Cooked Dog meme. Over one month, the tweet received over 291,000 likes and is currently the first known memetic use of the image.
